name: Route of the Montsant Refuges
name_original: Cueva de las Grallas - Vuelta circular por la Foradada
description: Hike the moderate 5.4-mile Cueva de las Grallas - Circular route via la Foradada, featuring a spectacular cave and natural rock formations.
region_intro: Hiking around Capafonts offers diverse terrain nestled between the Prades and Motllats mountains in Tarragona province. The region is characterized by a rugged, elevated environment with prominent peaks and stony landscapes featuring pine and oak forests. Capafonts is known for its abundant water sources, including 53 registered springs and the source of the Brugent river, which carves out picturesque valleys and ravines. Geological features such as Roca Foradada and the Cueva de las Gralles also define the area's natural landscape. Best hiking trails around Capafonts The most popular hiking route is Route of the Montsant Refuges, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 56 minutes to complete. This moderate path connects mountain refuges through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tolls de Les Tosques – Foradada Rock loop from Capafonts, a difficult 8.7 miles (14.1 km) path. This route explores river pools and the notable Roca Foradada rock formation. Local hikers also love the Tossal de la Baltasana summit – Roca Foradada of Prades loop from Capafonts, a 10.9 miles (17.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and past unique rock formations, often completed in about 5 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Capafonts is defined by mountainous terrain, abundant water sources, and unique rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Capafonts are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 290 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Capafonts's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Capafonts?
A: Capafonts offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging mountain ascents, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Capafonts?
A: Yes, Capafonts has several easy trails. For a relatively short and accessible option, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14891068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cova de les Gralles loop from Capafonts&lt;/a&gt;. This easy route is about 5 km long and takes around 1 hour 30 minutes, offering a pleasant walk through the local landscape.

Q: Where can I find waterfalls or significant water features on a hike in Capafonts?
A: Capafonts, meaning 'head of the fountains,' is rich in water sources. The source of the Brugent river, known as Fuente de la Nutria (Font de la Llúdriga), never runs dry and offers picturesque views of pools and waterfalls. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3575157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll de l'Olla Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/482088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eagle's Nest Natural Pool and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; for beautiful water features.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes starting directly from Capafonts?
A: Many trails in Capafonts are circular and start directly from the village. A challenging option that explores unique geological features is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6931064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tolls de Les Tosques – Foradada Rock loop from Capafonts&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 14 km long and takes around 4 hours 30 minutes.

Q: What kind of unique rock formations or caves can be explored while hiking in Capafonts?
A: The region is known for its fascinating geological features. Hikers can discover Roca Foradada, a natural rock formation, which is part of several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/265083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Cranes&lt;/a&gt; (Cueva de las Gralles) is another significant attraction, offering a spectacular cave experience. You can explore these on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19827697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tolls de Les Tosques – Cave of the Cranes loop from Capafonts&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Capafonts?
A: The trails in Capafonts are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 290 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the abundance of natural springs, and the unique rock formations that make the area a captivating outdoor destination.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Capafonts?
A: Yes, Capafonts offers several difficult routes for experienced hikers. For a demanding trek through mountainous terrain and past unique rock formations, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17511320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tossal de la Baltasana summit – Roca Foradada of Prades loop from Capafonts&lt;/a&gt;. This route is nearly 17.5 km long and typically takes over 5 hours to complete.

Q: What historical or cultural points of interest can be found along the hiking trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, hikers can encounter historical elements such as the 12th-century hermitage of the Virgen de Barrulles, located about 2 km from the village. Vestiges of the region's agricultural past, including old mills and threshing floors, can also be observed along various paths.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Capafonts?
A: Capafonts benefits from a cool and pleasant climate, which enhances its appeal for excursions. While specific seasons aren't detailed, the generally favorable climate suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for hiking, avoiding the hottest summer months and potential winter snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any long-distance or multi-day hiking options in the Capafonts area?
A: While many routes are day hikes, the region is part of a broader network. One notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e954157748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Montsant Refuges&lt;/a&gt;, which connects mountain refuges through varied terrain. This moderate 8.8 km trail can be a segment of a longer journey or a challenging day hike itself.

Q: Is Capafonts suitable for hiking with dogs?
A: The natural environment of Capafonts, with its diverse terrain and numerous water sources, generally makes it a welcoming area for hiking with dogs. However, always ensure your dog is well-behaved, kept on a leash where required, and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural beauty for everyone.

faqs.question: What makes the Cueva de las Grallas - Circular route via la Foradada a moderate hike?
faqs.answer: This circular route is considered moderate due to its length of approximately 8.76 km and an elevation gain of around 350 meters. While much of the path is accessible, some sections, especially those leading to the Cueva de las Grallas, can involve loose rock and steeper ascents. A good physical condition and sturdy, non-slip footwear are recommended.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cueva de las Grallas - Circular route via la Foradada?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Capafonts in Tarragona, Spain. While specific parking details aren't provided, hikers typically find parking in or near the village of Capafonts, which serves as a common starting point for routes in the Prades Mountains.

faqs.question: What are the main natural attractions along this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features several impressive natural landmarks. The highlight is the spectacular Grèvol Cave (Cueva de las Grallas), an enormous amphitheater-like formation. You'll also encounter the magnificent Foradada Rock, the Pont de Goi Natural Arch, and the Tolls de Les Tosques river area.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but it's particularly stunning after recent rains when a 15-meter-high waterfall cascades through the Cueva de las Grallas. During warmer months, the Tolls de Les Tosques offers a refreshing spot to cool off. Always check weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Cueva de las Grallas - Circular route via la Foradada dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs in the Muntanyes de Prades Natural Park or on this particular route are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Be prepared to carry out any waste.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Muntanyes de Prades park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for hiking the Cueva de las Grallas - Circular route via la Foradada or for accessing the Muntanyes de Prades Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations and signage.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cueva de las Grallas circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, starting with wide paths that transition into narrower, rockier, and steeper sections. You'll wind through lush forests and follow a river in parts. Inside the Cueva de las Grallas, you might need to duck in certain sections, and surfaces can be damp.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for damp or loose surfaces, sturdy, non-slip hiking boots are highly recommended. A flashlight is advisable for exploring inside the Cueva de las Grallas. Bring sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing layers for the weather.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails in the area. You'll find sections that overlap with routes such as Ruta dels Refugis del Montsant, El Brogit de la Vall, Camí de l'Escudelleta, Camí dels Motllats, and Camí de la Llódriga, among others.

faqs.question: Can I swim in any of the water features along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Tolls de Les Tosques is a river area along the route that is described as ideal for cooling off during warmer months. Always exercise caution and be aware of water levels and currents if you choose to enter the water.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Capafonts, a village in the Prades Mountains. While specific amenities directly at the trailhead are not guaranteed, Capafonts typically offers basic services, including cafes or restaurants, and potentially accommodation options for visitors to the area.
